text:
HEMATOPOIETIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT INPATIENT SERVICE
ATTENDING TRANSPLANT ONCOLOGY NOTE — DAY +21 / ENGRAFTMENT ASSESSMENT

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR NOTE:
Day +21 post-allogeneic peripheral blood stem cell transplantation. Formal engraftment assessment. Patient being evaluated for transition to outpatient care.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:

Ms. Renata Vasquez-Okonkwo is a 48-year-old female with ELN 2022 adverse-risk AML — specifically FLT3-ITD with a high allelic ratio (0.91), NPM1 wild-type, trisomy 8, and a complex co-mutation profile including DNMT3A R882H, TET2, WT1, NRAS, KDM6A, STAG2, and BCOR — who presented initially with leukocytosis to 87,400/uL, severe anemia, thrombocytopenia, and active gingival hemorrhage. She achieved MRD-negative complete remission (CR1) after a single cycle of 7+3 with midostaurin (per RATIFY protocol). Given her adverse-risk biology, she proceeded without further consolidation to myeloablative conditioning allo-HSCT from a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated male donor (MUD). She is now on day +21 following stem cell infusion.

Condensed transplant course to date:

Conditioning: She received fludarabine/busulfan x4 days (FluBu4), with busulfan pharmacokinetic dose adjustment on days 2–4 to target cumulative AUC within the 4800–6000 micromol*min/L range (actual AUC achieved within target). GVHD prophylaxis consists of tacrolimus (target trough 10–15 ng/mL, initiated day -3) and short-course MTX (MTX 15 mg/m2 on day +1, 10 mg/m2 on days +3 and +6). CMV prophylaxis: letermovir 480 mg daily (both donor and recipient are CMV seropositive, D+/R+). Antimicrobial prophylaxis: acyclovir, fluconazole, levofloxacin.

Day 0 infusion: Peripheral blood stem cell graft from 10/10 MUD, 34-year-old male. CD34+ cell dose 6.2 x 10^6/kg. Infusion was well-tolerated with standard pre-medications (diphenhydramine and hydrocortisone). No acute infusion reactions.

Early post-transplant course (days +1 through +20): As expected, the patient entered aplasia. Key complications during this period included:
- Grade 3 mucositis: Severe oral and esophageal mucositis beginning approximately day +7, requiring IV hydromorphone PCA and total parenteral nutrition. TPN initiated day +8 and maintained through day +16. She is now tolerating small amounts of soft foods, and the mucositis has improved markedly over the past 4–5 days. Pain is now managed with oral oxycodone as needed.
- Febrile neutropenia (day +8): Temperature to 38.9°C with ANC of 0/uL. Blood cultures (x4 bottles from PICC and peripheral sites) were all negative. She was already on levofloxacin prophylaxis and was upgraded to cefepime 2g IV q8h empirically. When she remained febrile through day +12 (4 days of antibacterials), empiric antifungal escalation was made to micafungin 150 mg IV daily in lieu of fluconazole. CT chest obtained on day +13 showed no pulmonary infiltrates, no halo sign, no nodular lesions — reassuringly clear. She defervesced by day +15.
- Grade 2 diarrhea: Present from approximately days +8 through +14, attributed to conditioning regimen toxicity and the Trexall given for GVHD prophylaxis. Stool studies (C. diff, bacterial culture, viral PCR) were negative. Managed with IV fluids and loperamide. Now resolved.
- Mild hepatotoxicity: Peak AST 78 U/L, ALT 92 U/L, bilirubin remained normal throughout. No clinical features of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (SOS/VOD) — no ascites, no right upper quadrant pain, no unexplained weight gain. Liver enzymes now trending back toward normal (today AST 41 U/L, ALT 54 U/L).
- Tacrolimus toxicity: No significant issues. Levels therapeutic throughout, troughs ranging 10.8–14.2 ng/mL. Creatinine peaked at 1.2 mg/dL on day +14 (mild, likely multifactorial — tacrolimus, aminoglycoside exposure, TPN) and has since returned to 0.88 mg/dL.

Engraftment:
- ANC first exceeded 500/uL on day +20 (ANC 610/uL); confirmed again today (day +21, ANC 820/uL). Neutrophil engraftment criteria met.
- Platelet engraftment (unsupported platelets >20,000/uL x2 consecutive days) is not yet formally met today but is expected imminently — today's platelet count is 34,000/uL without transfusion support over the past 48 hours.

Currently, the patient feels meaningfully better than she did one week ago. Her appetite is slowly returning. She is ambulating in the hallways with assistance and her energy is low but improving.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Constitutional: Persistent fatigue, improved. No fever today. No chills.
HEENT: Mild residual oral soreness, much improved from last week. Dry lips. No odynophagia now.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, no palpitations, no lower extremity edema.
Respiratory: No dyspnea at rest. Mild dyspnea with ambulation, attributable to deconditioning and anemia.
Gastrointestinal: Tolerating soft foods. No nausea today. Bowel movements soft, formed, 1–2/day. No bloody or melanotic stools.
Genitourinary: No dysuria, hematuria, or oliguria. Good urine output.
Musculoskeletal: Diffuse mild myalgia, improving. No joint swelling.
Dermatologic: No rash, no skin erythema, no blistering. Skin appears normal.
Neurologic: No headache, no confusion, no seizure activity. Mild fatigue-related cognitive dulling.
Psychiatric: Patient reports low mood but states she is coping. Has spoken with social work during this admission.

---

ALLERGIES: No known drug allergies.

MEDICATIONS (current inpatient):
1. Tacrolimus 1 mg IV twice daily (transitioning to oral today; target trough 10–15 ng/mL)
2. Letermovir 480 mg PO daily (CMV prophylaxis through day +100)
3. Micafungin 150 mg IV daily (empiric antifungal; plan to de-escalate to fluconazole now that fevers have resolved and CT chest negative)
4. Acyclovir 400 mg PO twice daily
5. Levofloxacin 500 mg PO daily
6. TMP-SMX DS 1 tab PO Monday/Wednesday/Friday (PCP prophylaxis started once ANC recovering)
7. Levothyroxine 88 mcg PO daily (pre-existing hypothyroidism)
8. Amlodipine 5 mg PO daily (pre-existing hypertension)
9. Oxycodone 5 mg PO q4–6h PRN pain (mucositis; step-down from IV hydromorphone PCA)
10. Ondansetron 4 mg PO q8h PRN nausea
11. Pantoprazole 40 mg PO daily
12. Docusate sodium 100 mg PO twice daily
13. Multivitamin PO daily

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. AML, FLT3-ITD (high allelic ratio), ELN 2022 adverse risk — as detailed above
2. Hypothyroidism — on levothyroxine
3. Mild hypertension — on amlodipine
No prior malignancies. No prior radiation. No prior surgeries other than bone marrow biopsy/PICC line placement during this hospitalization.

SOCIAL HISTORY: High school biology teacher. Married, two adult children. Non-smoker. No alcohol or illicit drug use. Strong family support — husband has been present throughout hospitalization.

FAMILY HISTORY: Mother with type 2 diabetes. Father with hypertension. No known family history of hematologic malignancy or solid tumors in first-degree relatives.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:

Vitals: Temperature 36.8°C (afebrile x 6 days), HR 88 bpm, BP 118/74 mmHg, RR 16, SpO2 97% on room air. Weight 63.2 kg (down ~3 kg from pre-conditioning baseline of 66.4 kg).

General: Fatigued-appearing but alert and oriented x3. Communicative and appropriate. In no acute distress.
HEENT: Mucous membranes moist (improved). Lips with mild residual chapping. Oral mucosa with scattered healing ulcerations, no frank necrosis. Gingiva appear healthy without bleeding. Oropharynx without exudate.
Neck: No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy palpable.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No JVD.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes or crackles.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender diffusely to deep palpation (likely residual from mucositis/GI toxicity). No hepatomegaly. Spleen not palpable (previously 16.4 cm splenomegaly at diagnosis, then 10.8 cm pre-transplant — appropriate regression). No ascites.
Extremities: Trace bilateral ankle edema (mild, likely from IV fluid resuscitation during febrile episode). No DVT signs. No petechiae or ecchymoses.
Skin: No rash. No maculopapular eruption. No skin thickening or blistering. PICC site is clean without erythema or discharge.
Neurologic: Alert and appropriate. Grossly intact strength in extremities. No focal deficits. Fine motor intact.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (Day +21):
CBC: WBC 1,900/uL | ANC 820/uL | Hemoglobin 9.1 g/dL (last PRBC transfusion day +17; transfusion-dependent) | Platelets 34,000/uL (unsupported x48h)
Differential: 43% neutrophils, 31% lymphocytes, 14% monocytes, 12% bands
Reticulocyte count: 1.4% (absolute 26,400/uL — rising, consistent with emerging erythroid engraftment)
Basic metabolic panel: Na 138, K 4.0, Cl 102, CO2 24, BUN 14, Creatinine 0.88 mg/dL (improved from peak 1.2), Glucose 98
Hepatic function: Total bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL, Direct bilirubin 0.2 mg/dL, AST 41 U/L, ALT 54 U/L, Alk Phos 112 U/L (all improving)
Magnesium 1.7 mg/dL (replete as needed; tacrolimus-related wasting)
Tacrolimus trough (drawn today, prior to AM dose): 11.4 ng/mL (therapeutic)
CMV PCR (plasma): Not detected
EBV PCR (plasma): Not detected

Peripheral blood STR chimerism (Day +21, resulted today):
- Overall chimerism: 97% donor
- CD3+ T-cell fraction: 89% donor
- CD33+ myeloid fraction: 99% donor
Interpretation: Robust early engraftment with essentially complete donor myeloid chimerism. T-cell chimerism at 89% is expected and appropriate at day +21; mixed T-cell chimerism of this degree is typical and does not raise concern for early graft failure. Trajectory will be monitored.

---

IMAGING:
CT chest (day +13, reviewed): No pulmonary infiltrates, no halo sign, no nodular lesions, no consolidation. Obtained during febrile period when fever source was unclear. Reassuringly negative.

---

ASSESSMENT / PLAN:

48-year-old woman with adverse-risk AML (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, NPM1 wild-type) in CR1 MRD-negative, now day +21 following myeloablative FluBu4 10/10 MUD allo-PBSCT. She has achieved neutrophil engraftment today (ANC >500/uL x2 days, first on day +20). Platelet engraftment pending formal criteria but appearing imminent given today's unsupported count of 34,000/uL.

1. ENGRAFTMENT — CONFIRMED (NEUTROPHIL). Peripheral blood chimerism demonstrates 99% donor myeloid chimerism and 97% overall chimerism. Results are excellent and consistent with robust, durable engraftment. T-cell chimerism at 89% is anticipated at this time point and will continue to rise with immune reconstitution. No concern for primary graft failure. Continue to monitor CBC daily.

2. DISEASE STATUS — Formal day +30 bone marrow biopsy/aspirate planned with MRD assessment (flow cytometry, FLT3-ITD PCR, WT1 quantitative PCR) and repeat chimerism. This will be the first formal post-transplant disease assessment.

3. IMMUNOSUPPRESSION — Tacrolimus 1 mg PO twice daily (converting from IV today now that oral absorption is reliable). Target trough 10–15 ng/mL. Continue tacrolimus and MTX course completed. Monitor tacrolimus level in 2–3 days after oral conversion. Short-course MTX prophylaxis course is complete.

4. INFECTION PROPHYLAXIS — Transitioning micafungin back to oral fluconazole 400 mg daily now that she has been afebrile for 6 days and CT chest was clear. Continue letermovir through day +100. Continue acyclovir. Continue levofloxacin through engraftment (can discontinue now that ANC >500). TMP-SMX three times weekly ongoing for PCP prophylaxis. Continue CMV monitoring weekly by PCR.

5. MUCOSITIS / NUTRITION — Mucositis resolved adequately to allow oral intake. TPN was stopped on day +16. Encourage oral intake with soft diet. Dietary consultation ongoing. Goal to advance to regular diet in next several days as tolerated.

6. ANEMIA — Hemoglobin 9.1 g/dL, transfusion-dependent at this time. Will transfuse PRBC for Hgb <8 g/dL. Reticulocyte count rising, suggesting emerging erythroid engraftment — anticipate transfusion independence over the next 1–2 weeks.

7. THROMBOCYTOPENIA — Platelet count 34,000/uL unsupported; trending upward. Transfuse for platelets <10,000/uL or <20,000/uL with active bleeding. No active bleeding currently.

8. PAIN / COMFORT — Oxycodone PRN for residual oral/pharyngeal discomfort. Reassess need in 24–48 hours; anticipate able to discontinue opioids soon given ongoing mucositis resolution.

9. ELECTROLYTES — Magnesium requiring supplementation; likely tacrolimus-related. Continue daily repletion as needed. Monitor potassium, creatinine closely.

10. GVHD SURVEILLANCE — No signs of acute GVHD at present. No rash, no diarrhea currently, no elevated bilirubin. Will monitor closely through month 3 (peak risk period for aGVHD). Patient and family educated on GVHD warning signs.

11. DISCHARGE PLANNING — Patient has met engraftment criteria. Medical status is appropriate for discharge to the outpatient transplant clinic with very close follow-up (initially 3x/week). Anticipated discharge: day +26 if clinical stability is maintained, PICC care is established at home, and reliable caregiver (husband confirmed) is in place. Patient lives within 30 minutes of our transplant center (meets distance requirement). Discharge medications, instructions, and emergency contact protocols will be reviewed with patient and husband prior to discharge.

12. PSYCHOSOCIAL — Patient has been understandably anxious and emotionally taxed throughout this lengthy hospitalization. Social work has been involved. She has good family support. Will continue support services in the outpatient setting and refer to survivorship/psycho-oncology as needed.

13. COMORBIDITIES — Continue levothyroxine (hypothyroidism) and amlodipine (hypertension). TSH to be checked at first outpatient visit. Blood pressure well-controlled throughout admission.

ECOG Performance Status: 2 (due to post-transplant fatigue, deconditioning, and anemia — expected to improve over next weeks to months).

Overall clinical trajectory: Favorable. This patient has demonstrated excellent early graft function with full myeloid donor chimerism at day +21. The complications she experienced — febrile neutropenia, mucositis, GI toxicity — were all expected and have resolved or are resolving without lasting sequelae. The next major milestone will be the day +30 bone marrow assessment confirming MRD-negative remission in the post-transplant setting.

Time spent: 60 minutes (includes review of records, examination, and family discussion).

Attending Physician, Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplant Service
